description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a black metal gable roof and a prominent central chimney. The front facade features two wooden double doors and several multi-pane windows, with a covered porch extending along one side. The brickwork is a deep red with visible weathering, and the windows are framed in white, providing a classic, historic appearance. The roof has a stepped design, with one section higher than the other, and the porch roof is corrugated metal. The house sits on a rectangular concrete base, and the rear side includes a single door and utility elements. The overall design suggests a late 19th or early 20th-century American vernacular style.
description: A two-story red brick house with a black gable roof, white-trimmed windows, and a covered porch. The facade features double wooden doors and a central chimney, evoking a classic, historic look.
description_view_0: This view shows the main facade with two wooden double doors, three windows, and the covered porch extending to the right.
description_view_1: This view highlights the side of the house with the porch roof, a single door, and two windows above, as well as the central chimney.
description_view_2: This view displays the longer side wall with three windows and a single door, emphasizing the stepped roof and brickwork.
description_view_3: This rear view shows a single window, utility elements, and the chimney, with the porch roof visible on the left.
attribute_entryway_design: Double wooden doors with a covered porch and corrugated metal roof, no steps, and minimal decorative elements.
